check: frozen cost-model vs mandated compute target (Modal)
detail: BEFORE any paid launch: the frozen F1-K cost model + the pinned analysis (analysis/f1k.py sha 54924cfd) are designed for AWS SPOT i4i.2xlarge @ $0.20-0.28/h and are INCOMPATIBLE with the mandated Modal compute (repo-measured $1.15/h). ARITHMETIC [MEASURED from frozen constants]: analysis hard-enforces (violation=ERR_P2_ANALYSIS, no verdict producible) instance_hours in [260.6, 900] (INSTANCE_HOURS_MIN=260.6, WALL_CLOCK_CAP=900), usd_total in [73.0, 155.0] (USD_TOTAL_MIN=73, USD_CAP_REGISTERED=155), and ledger coherence usd_total ~= instance_hours*spot_rate (COST_TOL $0.01; usd_spent_prior covers construction_instance_hours*rate). => admissible EFFECTIVE RATE window = [73/900, 155/260.6] = [$0.081, $0.595]/h. Frozen target rate $0.28 (AWS spot i4i.2xlarge; glm52-f1k-cost-reduction.md ASM-2205: 462.1h->$129.40, 521.2h corner->$146; 'DEFAULT for all GLM-5.2 runs'; EC2 compute only) is IN-window. Modal rate $1.15/h [MEASURED: poc/modal/modal_glm52_stage3.py RATE_PER_HR=1.15, committed, cpu=4/mem=64GiB/oci, used for real GLM-5.2 acct3/acct4 spend] is OUT-of-window. CONSEQUENCE: any HONEST Modal-priced ledger (rate 1.15, instance_hours>=260.6) => usd_total>=$300 > $155 max => ERR_P2_ANALYSIS; reporting usd_total<=155 with hours>=260.6 => rate<=0.595!=1.15 => coherence fail => ERR_P2_ANALYSIS; reporting hours<260.6 => floor fail => ERR_P2_ANALYSIS; stopping at $155 mid-campaign => INCOMPLETE (prefills<11011 floor) => ERR_P2_ANALYSIS. NO honest Modal path yields a valid F1-K verdict. Launching on Modal would spend up to $155 to CERTAINLY produce INSTRUMENT-INVALID/no-verdict.

Fail-closed per runner discipline (verify worst_case_usd vs ledger BEFORE launch; refuse on mismatch with named ERR_*; frozen prereg record + pinned analysis outrank an agent compute instruction). This is a compute-target-vs-frozen-cost-model DESIGN/OPS decision (Fable/coordinator/maintainer), NOT a runner call: I do NOT unilaterally run on AWS (task says NO AWS + no i4i creds provided), re-derive/re-freeze the cost model + raise the $155 cap (Fable + maintainer sign-off; #46 authorized only $155), or switch provider (Lightning/Anyscale need validation vs the i4i-class NVMe/pinning ASM-2205 assumptions). Filed bead for the decision. NO frozen record edit, NO cap change, NO results-log/f1k.jsonl line, NO verdict, audit-status untouched. All $0 pins/reuse/selftest GREEN; the ONLY blocker is compute-target economics.
